one_law: One accountable manager, one specialist per fault domain, and no omnipotent agent: the Issue Manager may never silently classify, patch, verify, approve, and merge its own work (separation of duties).
not_active_note: PROPOSED role map. These canonical role IDs are NOT active authority until SG ratifies NF-UNIFIED-MOTOR-ISSUE-CI-OWNERSHIP-V1 and the founder merges the operationalization PR. Until then this registry declares intent + evidence, not live routing.

roles.title: NOOS Portfolio Continuation Owner
roles.implementation: existing NOOS continuation owner; hosts the issue-manager and ci-reliability-owner capabilities/profiles below (not a new cloud agent)
responsibilities: persistent continuation ownership of the NOOS portfolio
parent identity for the Issue Manager and CI Reliability Owner capabilities

roles.title: NOOS Issue Manager
roles.implementation: capability/profile of noetfield:noos.portfolio-owner initially; NOT a sixth independent cloud agent
responsibilities: GitHub issue and PR-failure intake
deduplication into one canonical issue record
severity and blast-radius classification
fault-domain classification and ownership resolution
stale-issue monitoring
assignment to the correct Motor recipe or owner
escalation to the founder only when authority is genuinely required
confirming evidence-based closure (a green rerun alone does not close a flaky/unknown-cause issue)
does_not: self-approve, self-verify, or merge its own routed work (separation of duties)

roles.title: NOOS CI Reliability Owner
roles.implementation: profile under NOOS initially; not necessarily another core agent
responsibilities: monitor required checks and detect flaky checks
compare baseline and candidate results
distinguish infrastructure failures from code failures
inspect workflow logs
maintain CI health and retry policy
open a bounded repair job for genuine CI-system faults
track failure recurrence
produce CI incident receipts
roles.founder_or_sg_required_when: a workflow edit changes permissions, secrets, environments, branch triggers, or production targets

roles.title: SANDBOX Builder Owner
responsibilities: owns candidate-caused CI repair execution
delegates the actual repair to a bounded worker in an isolated branch/worktree
reruns the exact failed check, commits, and updates the PR
worker_adapters: openai.codex-cloud (worker adapter, NOT the institutional CI owner)

roles.title: SourceA Dispatch Owner
responsibilities: compiles the bounded repair job from the Issue Manager's classified + routed issue
binds required inputs before execution (missing input = job invalid at creation)

failure_class_routing.failure_class: stale test contract from an intentional code change
formatting, lint, type, or build error
runner outage or GitHub infrastructure failure
secret missing, expired, or incorrectly scoped

failure_class_routing.owner: NOOS observes and retries per policy; no code patch by default
STOP and escalate; do not guess

separation_of_duties.issue_manager: decides what the problem is and who owns it
separation_of_duties.ci_reliability_owner: owns CI-system faults and flaky infrastructure
separation_of_duties.verifier: reviews only when risk justifies it
separation_of_duties.founder: approves consequential authority or promotion changes
